questions.question: Which of the following best defines a political party?

options.A: A group of people sharing similar interests
options.B: A group of people who contest elections to gain political power
options.C: A group of voters in a region

questions.explanation: A political party is a group of people who contest elections and aim to hold political power.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of a political party?
questions.explanation: Judges are not part of political parties; parties consist of leaders, active members, and followers.

questions.question: Which function of political parties involves presenting policies and programmes?
questions.explanation: Political parties present policies and programmes during elections.

questions.question: What role do political parties play in legislatures?
questions.explanation: Members of legislatures generally follow party policies while making laws.

questions.question: Which party system is characterized by two dominant parties?
questions.explanation: Two-party systems have two major parties dominating elections.

questions.question: Which of the following countries is an example of a two-party system?
questions.explanation: The United States follows a two-party system dominated by Democrats and Republicans.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of a multiparty system?

options.C: It ensures only one party rules

questions.explanation: Multiparty systems allow representation of diverse groups and interests.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a coalition government?

options.A: Government formed by a single party
options.B: Government formed by alliance of several parties

questions.explanation: Coalition governments are formed when several parties join together to form government.

questions.question: Which organization is responsible for registering political parties in India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission registers political parties.

questions.question: Which factor determines whether a party is recognized as a national party?
questions.explanation: National party recognition depends on vote share and seats won in elections.

questions.question: Which political parties mainly focus on regional issues?
questions.explanation: State parties represent regional interests.

questions.question: Which of the following challenges relates to power being concentrated among a few leaders?
questions.explanation: Lack of internal democracy means decision-making is limited to a few leaders.

questions.question: Which challenge arises when political leadership passes within a family?
questions.explanation: Dynastic succession occurs when leadership passes within a family.

questions.question: Which of the following affects fairness of elections due to financial influence?
questions.explanation: Money power can influence elections unfairly.

questions.question: Which law was introduced to prevent elected representatives from changing parties?
questions.explanation: The anti-defection law prevents representatives from switching parties.

questions.question: Which of the following institutions monitors election activities in India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission monitors elections and political parties.

questions.question: Which function of political parties helps citizens connect with the government?
questions.explanation: Political parties help citizens communicate with government authorities.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the role of media in political reforms?
questions.explanation: Media helps monitor political activities and inform citizens.

questions.question: Why do political parties form alliances during elections?
questions.explanation: Parties form alliances to strengthen their chances of winning elections.

questions.question: Which of the following reflects social divisions in politics?
questions.explanation: Different parties often represent different social groups.

questions.question: Which of the following is an important role of opposition parties?
questions.explanation: Opposition parties criticize government policies and provide alternatives.

questions.question: Which of the following ensures transparency in elections?
questions.explanation: Disclosure of candidate details ensures transparency.

questions.question: Which concept refers to peaceful transfer of power through elections?
questions.explanation: Democratic stability allows peaceful change of government through elections.

questions.question: Which role of political parties involves planning government policies?
questions.explanation: Political parties formulate policies based on their ideology.

questions.question: Which of the following reforms can strengthen political parties?
questions.explanation: Transparency and internal democracy strengthen political parties.
